title: Health Determinants and Health Equity
prerequisites: Chapter 1: Introduction to Global Public Health

learning_objectives.text: Define key concepts including social determinants of health, health equity, and structural determinants
Explain the WHO Commission on Social Determinants of Health conceptual framework and its components
Apply determinants of health frameworks to analyze health disparities in different global contexts
Analyze complex causal pathways between social, environmental, and economic determinants and health outcomes
Evaluate evidence for interventions addressing upstream social determinants of health
Design comprehensive strategies to address health inequities using multi-sectoral approaches

case_studies.title: United Kingdom's Health Inequalities Strategy: Addressing the Social Gradient
case_studies.focus: Comprehensive national approach to health inequalities
key_lessons: Importance of political commitment, cross-government action, local implementation

case_studies.title: Brazil's Bolsa Família: Conditional Cash Transfers and Health Equity
case_studies.focus: Social protection program addressing multiple determinants
key_lessons: Intersectoral coordination, targeting vulnerable populations, measurable outcomes

case_studies.title: Urban-Rural Health Disparities in Sub-Saharan Africa
case_studies.focus: Geographic inequities in health access and outcomes
key_lessons: Infrastructure, service delivery models, equity measurement

case_studies.title: Indigenous Health Determinants in High-Income Countries
key_lessons: Historical trauma, cultural safety, community-led approaches
